Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.overview: Most Chapter 19 categories require a 7th character (A, D, S). Traumatic fractures have additional 7th character options.
Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.assignment_basis: Assign based on whether the patient is receiving active treatment, not on whether the provider is new.
Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.complication_codes_active_treatment: Active treatment refers to treatment for the condition described by the code (e.g., T84.50XA for prosthetic joint infection while being actively treated).
Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.A - initial encounter: Use for each encounter where the patient is receiving active treatment for the condition.
Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.D - subsequent encounter: Use after active treatment is completed and the patient is receiving routine care during healing/recovery.
Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.aftercare_Z_codes: Do not use aftercare Z codes for injuries/poisonings when 7th characters identify subsequent care; instead use the acute injury code with 7th character 'D'.
Application of 7th Characters in Chapter.S - sequela: Use for complications/conditions that arise as a direct result of a prior condition. Sequence the sequela (e.g., scar) first, followed by the injury code with 7th character 'S'.

Coding of Injuries.separate_codes: Assign separate codes for each injury unless a combination code exists.
Coding of Injuries.avoid_T07_in_inpatient: Do not assign T07 (Unspecified multiple injuries) in inpatient settings unless no specific information is available.
Coding of Injuries.exclude_normal_surgical_wounds: Do not use traumatic injury codes (S00–T14.9) for normal, healing surgical wounds or complications of surgical wounds.
Coding of Injuries.sequencing: Sequence the most serious injury first, as determined by the provider and focus of treatment.
Coding of Injuries.Superficial injuries: Do not code superficial injuries (abrasions/contusions) when associated with more severe injuries of the same site.
Coding of Injuries.Primary injury with damage to nerves/blo: Sequence the primary injury first, then add codes for nerve (e.g., S04) and/or vessel (e.g., S15) injuries. If the primary injury is to nerves or vessels, sequence that first.
Coding of Injuries.Iatrogenic injuries: Do not assign injury codes for injuries occurring during/as a result of medical intervention; assign appropriate complication codes.

Coding of Traumatic Fractures.multiple_coding_principles: Follow multiple-coding principles; code specified sites individually per S02, S12, S22, S32, S42, S49, S52, S59, S62, S72, S79, S82, S89, S92 and documentation detail.
Coding of Traumatic Fractures.defaults: If not indicated, default to closed fracture and displaced fracture.

Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.initial: Use appropriate 7th character for initial encounter (A/B/C) for active treatment, including delayed presentation/nonunion initially treated.
Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.subsequent: Use appropriate 7th character for subsequent care after active treatment during healing/recovery.
Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.surgical_complications: Complications during healing after fracture repair are coded with appropriate complication codes.
Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.malunion_nonunion: Report subsequent care with nonunion (K, M, N) or malunion (P, Q, R). If treatment was delayed for the fracture/nonunion, assign initial-encounter 7th character.
Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.Gustilo_open_fracture: For forearm, femur, lower leg/ankle, if Gustilo type not specified, assign 7th character for type I or II (B, E, H, M, Q).
Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.osteoporosis_fracture: Use M80 (not traumatic fracture code) for fractures in patients with known osteoporosis when mechanism would not fracture healthy bone.
Initial vs. subsequent encounter for fra.aftercare: Do not use aftercare Z codes; assign acute fracture code with appropriate 7th character for aftercare of traumatic fractures.

Coding of Traumatic Fractures.Multiple fractures sequencing: Sequence based on severity of fractures.
Coding of Traumatic Fractures.Physeal fractures: Assign only the code identifying the physeal fracture type; do not add a separate specific bone code.

Coding of Burns and Corrosions.definitions: Burns: thermal (except sunburn), electricity, radiation. Corrosions: chemical burns. Same guidelines apply.
Coding of Burns and Corrosions.classification: Current burns (T20–T25) classified by depth, extent, and agent (external cause). Eye/internal organ burns (T26–T28) classified by site only.

Sequencing of burn and related condition.highest_degree_first: Sequence the highest-degree burn first when multiple burns are present.
Sequencing of burn and related condition.external_multiple_burns: For treatment of external multiple burns, sequence highest-degree burn first.
Sequencing of burn and related condition.internal_vs_external: When both internal and external burns exist, principal diagnosis depends on admission circumstances.
Sequencing of burn and related condition.burns_with_related_conditions: When burns and related conditions (e.g., smoke inhalation, respiratory failure) are present, principal diagnosis depends on admission circumstances.

Coding of Burns and Corrosions.Burns of the same anatomic site: Classify to the subcategory for the highest degree at the site/side (e.g., T24.311- for 2nd and 3rd degree right thigh).
Coding of Burns and Corrosions.Non-healing burns: Code as acute burns; necrosis of burned skin = non-healed burn.
Coding of Burns and Corrosions.Infected burn: Assign an additional code for the infection.
Coding of Burns and Corrosions.Assign separate codes for each burn site: Assign separate codes per site. Avoid T30 (unspecified body region) unless sites are not specified. Use 'multiple sites' only if individual sites not documented.

Extent of body surface involved (T31/T32.usage: Use for acute burns/corrosions when site not specified or for additional data (e.g., burn unit reporting, ≥20% third-degree). Not for sequelae.
Extent of body surface involved (T31/T32.rule_of_nines: Head/neck 9%, each arm 9%, each leg 18%, anterior trunk 18%, posterior trunk 18%, genitalia 1%; adjust for infants/children/body habitus as needed.

Coding of Burns and Corrosions.Encounters for sequela of burns: Use burn/corrosion code with 7th character 'S' for late effects (e.g., scars, contractures).
Coding of Burns and Corrosions.Sequelae with a late effect code and cur: It may be appropriate to assign both a current burn (7th 'A'/'D') and a sequela burn code (7th 'S') on the same record.
Coding of Burns and Corrosions.External cause code with burns/corrosion: Use external cause codes to identify source/intent/place of occurrence.

Adverse Effects, Poisoning, Underdosing.combination_codes: T36–T65 codes combine substance and intent; no additional external cause code required.
Adverse Effects, Poisoning, Underdosing.Do not code directly from the Table of D: Always confirm in the Tabular List.
Adverse Effects, Poisoning, Underdosing.Same code for multiple events: If same code describes the causative agent for multiple events, assign once.
Adverse Effects, Poisoning, Underdosing.Multiple substances: Code each substance unless a combination code exists; for multiple unspecified substances use T50.91-.

Drug toxicity classifications.Adverse Effect: First code the nature of the adverse effect, then the drug adverse effect code (T36–T50 with 5th/6th character '5').

Poisoning.sequence: First assign T36–T50 poisoning code with intent (accidental, intentional self-harm, assault, undetermined); if unknown, default to accidental; 'undetermined' only if explicitly documented.
Poisoning.add_manifestations: Use additional codes for all manifestations.
examples: Prescription/admin error by any person → poisoning.
Nonprescribed drug taken with correctly prescribed/administered drug causing reaction → poisoning.
references: See Section I.C.4. for insulin pump malfunctions.

Underdosing.definition: Taking less than prescribed or discontinuing against medical advice; code T36–T50 with 5th/6th character '6'.
Underdosing.documentation: No change in condition required; documentation of reduced/stopped dose is sufficient.
Underdosing.principal_rule: Never principal/first-listed. If relapse/exacerbation occurs, code the medical condition.
Underdosing.intent_modifiers: Use Z91.12-, Z91.13-, Z91.14-, Z91.A4- for noncompliance, or Y63.6–Y63.9 for complication of care, as applicable.

Toxic Effects.definition: Harmful substance exposure (ingested/skin contact) → categories T51–T65.
Toxic Effects.sequence: Assign toxic effect code first, then all associated manifestations.
Toxic Effects.intent: Codes carry intent (accidental, intentional self-harm, assault, undetermined).

Adult and child abuse, neglect and other.sequencing: Sequence first T74 (confirmed) or T76 (suspected), then any mental health or injury codes.
Adult and child abuse, neglect and other.confirmed_vs_suspected: If documentation states abuse/neglect → confirmed (T74.-); if documented as suspected → T76.-.
Adult and child abuse, neglect and other.external_cause_perpetrator: For confirmed cases, add assault external cause (X92–Y09) and perpetrator code (Y07) if known. Do not add these for suspected cases.
Adult and child abuse, neglect and other.reference: See Section I.C.15. Abuse in a pregnant patient.

Complications of care.Pain due to medical devices: Assign appropriate Chapter 19 T code for device-related pain; add G89.18 (acute) or G89.28 (chronic) to specify pain type.

Transplant complications.Transplants other than kidney: Use T86- for complications/rejection affecting function of the transplanted organ, plus a secondary code identifying the complication. Do not code pre-existing/unrelated conditions as complications unless they affect function.
references: See I.C.21. transplant organ removal status.
See I.C.2. malignant neoplasm associated with transplanted organ.
See I.C.1.d.4. sepsis due to infection in transplanted organ.
Transplant complications.Kidney transplant complications: Assign T86.1- for documented kidney transplant complications (failure, rejection, other). Do not assign T86.1- solely for CKD post-transplant unless a complication is documented. If unclear, query. For conditions affecting the transplanted kidney (other than CKD), assign T86.1- plus a secondary code for the complication. For CKD post-transplant without complication, see I.C.14.
kidney_references: See I.C.14. CKD and kidney transplant status.
See I.C.1.d.4. sepsis sequencing in transplanted organ infection.

Complications of care.Complication codes that include the exte: Some T codes incorporate the external cause and procedure type; no additional external cause code is needed.
Complications of care.Complications of care codes within the b: Use intraoperative/postprocedural complication codes from body system chapters first, followed by specific complication code(s), unless the index directs to a Chapter 19 T code.
